author: @dismayedtrinket2518
text:
I genuinely appreciate how cleanly and concisely you have parsed this.

A content creator can and should excercise their ability to curate and foster a community if that is what they want to do. If the trolls really want to say what they want they can go make their own YouTube channel, which we can all promptly ignore. Free speech doesn't mean we have to listen.

author: @jimadams7765
text: I feel that You Tube creators should stop being reluctant to name and shame these people. It would help us as "normal" commentators to avoid them on this or other sites. I realise that there is this "free speech" paradigm but I'm not suggesting we prevent that. Just do it somewhere else please. Preferably on their own site. And we all know why that doesn't happen? Because they wouldn't get any views. Good lesson for them, then.

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
Fortunately there are people,  like yourself, not enough though, to redress the balance. Thanks Rob. Thanks very much.
Most really/deliberately rude people are usually easy to spot, they wear G4S uniforms, work at Job-centres, or council offices; especially the ones who claim to be 'managers', just peel back their very loose facade/lies of respectability/honesty/integrity/competence, and there it is in spades.
I don't know if the system actually creates them, or they are naturally predisposed and gravitate to the opportunity to massage their egos.
There are others, but the above are often a good indicator.
A good test is to suggest they might be wrong, then watch the denials/lies and SHTF in your direction.
Unfortunately you can't always 'just walk away' when they are your only route to a particular issue, or their facade/lies etc has been exposed and they are then abusing position/procedure in a determined manner to punish/harrass/destroy you.

author: @jamesross1003
text: Thank you for this message Rob!  There is a difference in being critical and being rude.  I should hope that if anyone should feel that my comments come across as rude that they would call me out for it.  No one is perfect.  It is sad though that it is even a needed topic of discussion.  A friendly debate sure I'll go for it, a belittling not so much.  You have to be openminded for a conversation to be of value.  Keep the great videos coming!  Anything on the graphene oxide developments(for example the nano coating the industry is touting as a graphene protectant for surfaces) that you might could educate us on?  I have been curious since first hearing about it and wondering to what other uses it might have.  Also a bit apprehensive as to whether it really is worth using as say a coating for auto-glass windshields ect.  Anyway, I digress,  have a wonderful day.  Words to live by....."Don't feed the trolls!".  Looking forward to whatever experiment you get up to next.

I think people need to be reminded of how to act appropriately, and respectfully, in providing their comments. You have such a lovely way of communicating - you can use your channel to provide some guidance.

author: @chrisgriffiths2533
text:
Robert You Raise the Tricky Issue of Commentary and Opinions with Many Facets.
However Recent History Shows You Bravely took on a Tricky Issue.  You Promoted Burning, Mostly Oils or Derivatives of Oils, You did this for Good Reasons.
Nevertheless Robert Your Burning Promotions had Many Facets and I was Concerned about a Number of Known Negative Outcomes.
For Example, I have a Friend Who Burnt Himself Badly as a Child Putting Petrol onto a Fire.  This is Long ago, but He Was Copying a Practice of Many Men at that Time.
For Example, Many People have been Gased to Death in Their Homes Burning things for Heat.
Etc Etc.

Anyway back to Respect Etc, Firstly Along comes the Internet and Many of Us Know the Potential of the Internet to Progress Humanity.  YouTube is a Subset of this Potential.
However it's My View that YouTube is a Public Arena, this comes with Challenges which Includes the Fact that No One can Operate in the Public Arena without Scrutiny.
However at this Stage the Internet is Still a Work in Progress and there are Many Problems to be Solved.  One of the Worst of these is Criminal Activity on the Internet, Big Problem.
A Second Problem is Differences of Opinions.
Third Problem is the Nuances of Communication.
Fourth Problem is the Variences in Human Communication Capabilities, Differing Language Backgrounds, English Language Confusions and Variences in Age, IQ and Ethics.
So to Me the Respect Issue is Always a Two Way Street and I Think Some People Use the Issue of Respect as a Cop Out.    The Same as They will Use Any Other Thing to Justify what They Often Don't Understand.
Of Course Perfect Human Behaviour All of the Time is a Worthy Pursuit but Very Difficult to Achieve.

Again Robert,  I Thank You for Putting Much Quality Knowledge into the Public Arena.

author: @12thsonofisrael
text:
Good job 👍😊🆗👌👏

Sometimes It is necessary to take steps to maintain some resemblance of decorum, and indeed you have done well to take a few minutes of your valuable time to make it so. 

A few decades ago when I used monitor a chat forum and some idiot just would not  cheque his anarchy at the door, finally, I would message him with this, 
"You have tried the will and patience of the benevolent and powerful yet most forgiving, to the point of exacting their revenge, and you will learn, 'revenge is a dish best when served cold and quickly'. You are banned for eternity or longer if we can find a method to do so! Good-bye until then. LOL 😆" 

Yes, that is exactly what I did and still don't regret it. 
If you allow the anarchy, the miscreants will even lay waste to the very pillars of your home.  😏

author: @malcolmnew8973
text: Hi Robert. He clearly upset you, which upsets me to be honest. I have been following you for some time and gained a wealth of information (+entertainment, humour etc!) from your YouTube videos. I am (probably!) a similar age to you and brought up to be polite and respectful but there is always someone in any community who goes out of their way to be rude and disruptive. This is nothing new, I recommend a book by M Scott Peck called "The different drum (community making and peace)", very pre-internet but still valid in the web based communities we are building now, if you find the time. Keep going and don't let the snow trolls get you down!

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
Rick3y Rick3y  Thank You very Much for your kind reply.
I know that this time it really wasn't me!!
( As That Troll was nasty towards me as well!!)

But, I do know that sometimes my sense of Humour r even just the way I write things, 
maybe ,can upset folk!

So I just wanted to let folk know that if I seem to be out of order, 
that it's Okay to let me know!!  

Also, I really want to say a Big Thank You very Much for your Kind reply to my comment,
 I appreciate it more than I can express, seriously. 

Have a great day and Thank you very much for taking the time to write to me!!🙂💞

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
Rick3y Rick3y Thank you very much, People like yourself, James Cunliffe and Even Robert,(Lol!)
Are the reason I keep coming Here to learn!!

It  also helps me regain my confidence in myself,
 I sometimes ask silly questions, due to the traumatic brain injury...


So it is helpful that I can talk to good folks,like yourself,,
 Knowing  that I will be treated as an equal
And if Im a bit daft,  the decent people here 
help me to grasp new consepts, 
laughing With me, which is always nice!!

There have been other channels Ive watched, then after asking something,,,
 Ive been laughed At...
Thats very demoralising,,
 ~a bit like when I was the first woman at the local college, to do the Mechanics Course!!

Where even the Tutors were bigots ,
not taking me seriously when I had to make a complaint about a fellow students very bad behaviour!!

(Eventually, I was Forced to make use of a large Torque Wrench to a "Delicate" region!) 
It was rather  upsetting to have to deal with someone in this way!!

I suffer  from Sub-Clinical mania , from the TBI, which does make me a Chatterbox,as you can probably see,Lol!

Thats another reason Im so grateful for this awesome Community of  amazing, inspirational,,,,
"Thinkers N Tinkerers" here!!

(Sorry,,,,,I Do Go On....)🙂
I suppose I might have gone straight to the point, n said,"Thank You!"!!🙂😄

I only sponsored one program on YouTube and it is this one.  Keep up the great work.

text:
It's not that difficult to refrain from rudeness.

That way, should I have a knee jerk reaction

web.

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text:
Ouch...  I tend to see folks like that (those who are so in a consistent way, not just having a bad day) as wounded--they often don't really truly love themselves and tend to be rather unhappy deep down.  

    Dunno, but the direct nonphysical/spiritual experiences I've had, have led me to the awareness that we are all interconnected at a fundamental level, and that what we do, think, don't do, don't think, etc to any others, is something we do, think, don't think, etc in relation to ourselves and to the whole. Thus, it is hard for me to think of anyone as completely worthless or even worse than same. * (Btw, I think the above relates a lot to a combo of quantum entanglement, Bell's theorem, and non locality, if someone needs a scientific reference/context). 

      With all that said, I'm all for banning or time outs if people won't change their behavior for the better.  Consequences can help people to grow up and mature, and that's what the above type of folks need to do. And, some individuals can't be reached no matter what others do for them (particularly common with folks on the ASPD spectrum aka psychopaths and sociopaths).

author: @totherarf
text:
This is something that concerns me ..... and I worry that I am sometimes guilty of pushing my point across others objections.

One of the problems is the media. Typing a message strips it of all the nuance of a face to face conversation. People can take the wrong meaning from a comment, but I guess that is not what you are talking about here. 

Personally I have a set of views that I perceive as correct and act upon them, eather in defence or extending them. But, (and much like my own) it is a Big But ..... if I hear convincing counter argument I am more than willing to change my mind on things. I think that is a key element that may be missing in some!

I suspect that the relative anonymity of the web gives people the false feeling that they will not face any consequences for what they say, so they say it Big and Loud. Also you get crowd mentality seeping in. People in groups (especially large ones) will talk, act and even think differently than they do on their own. The web allows people to assume they are in a group and that group is aligned with their own actions, when in reality they are not! 

It is nice to be important, but more important to be nice!

author: @Electrodoc1968
text:
Hi Rob.

i'd posted this earlier on a very busy comment section in one of your older uploaded videos and I don't think it would of been noticed so this may seem of topic but this upload is only a few minutes old so i thought i'd post it here..

I've had an idea for a while now and i'm excited to share it with someone, such as yourself who may be able to give it a test.?
The idea is to have A really really long pipe. ie a drain pipe 
With A vacuum cleaner turbine fan at the bottom of the pipe or maybe a few of them on a single axle aranged 1 on top of the previous one.
The air inlet at the bottom would have side air inlets so a shaft of threaded bar can be implemented as a free spinning bearinged axle mount for the turbine(s)  to attach to.


This would allow for the turbine(s)  to spin freely within the pipe and the turbine(s)  are shrouded by the pipe walls with a small gap between the turbines body and the pipe wall to allow for air flow to continue towards the lower pressure at the top of the pipe and towards the next turbine up.
(I don't know how high up the pipe would need to be to get enough pressure difference)..
Possibly a 5 story block.?

I do know that vacuum cleaner turbines are EXTREMELY Efficient and if you remove one from a cleaner completely and balance the centre hole on your fingertip and lightly blown into the central inlet they spin very well.

The idea is to allow the difference in air pressure from the bottom of the pipe & the top of the pipe to cause airflow through the turbines central port and out of the turbines sides having passed through the  vanes within it's very efficiently shaped Ventura and streamlined blades.

Attach magnets, coils etc etc.  :)

I'd construct it myself but the council are already moaning at me for having 5 microwave ovens, 4 vacuum cleaners, 3 broken washers, 2 four stroke mowers, and a cartridge of CO2 Gas.
(Yes 12 days of Christmas tune works with that scrap list).  Plus i'm suffering with many ailments..

Hopefully this is relatively clear I tried to draw a description with ASCI Characters but gave up when it began to look like something non-family friendly..  :)
I could do a better diagram but i'd need to share on google drive and create the drawing. 
Let me know what you reckon.
Cheers Richard.

author: @sarahlange1002
text:
Fair enough.
Suggestion: I just made a 33.8oz massive candle using a glass jar and 33.8oz of shortening that cost me $3.65. I put a 4mm wick in it and it was burned for 125 hours and was enough to light an average sized room sufficiently to see everyone in the room and cast shadows on every wall.
The 4mm wick is more that twice the width of the average store bought candle.
If used for 6 hours per day, my $3.65 candle will last for almost 3 weeks.
Felt compelled to let you know because perhaps it'll make a good mention or discussion in future videos?

replies.author: @ProlificInvention
replies.text: That's a great idea, an alternative is an old LED car headlight that is connected in parallel to -2- 18650 batteries.  For some reason I've found the electronics allow the light to provide usable light to a room for 21 continuous days.  I made a video on it at one point.  I've subsequently found an old style LED flashlight (with many bulbous style individual LEDs) that runs on 18650 batteries will provide usable light continuously for more than two weeks.

replies.author: @NM-wd7kx
replies.text:
I love the idea of a large terracotta pot heater done with a giant candle.

replies.author: @colfaxschuyler3675
replies.text:
I've found that, in extreme cold conditions, a freshly killed tauntaun will provide much needed warmth for several hours, even with ambient temperatures down to -60 C.  
The bad part being, you have to split them open and climb inside.

replies.author: @colfaxschuyler3675
replies.text: @ProlificInvention If you have "Dollar Tree" stores in your location, they periodically sell a string of 10 "droplet" LEDs on a double AA battery case.  The string is very bright initially, but as the battery voltage diminishes, the LEDs draw less current.  They turn into glowing dots good for area indicators and ambiance.  They appear to be able to run on micro amps, and their glow will last for several weeks on a pair of NiMH batteries.

replies.author: @ProlificInvention
replies.text: @colfaxschuyler3675 I have noticed that also if you replace that crappy battery they have in there with a high quality nimh battery fully charged they're way better.  Another thing to try is go to their flashlight section and see if they have some old stock LED flashlights (bulbous type) or sometimes those little lanterns you could try or even the "light switch LED lights with 2 18650s fully charged and wired in parallel with a mini DC-DC Step down converter.  I bet if you used better componentry then I tested you could have a month of usable light of over a few candlepower

author: @C-M-E
text:
Kind of makes you wonder if those folks who envisioned the entirety of the internet (based on the idea of more readily exchanging ideas and information freely across the world) fully knew what kind of mess it could become 'out in the wild', especially once those of small-mindedness and large ego discovered the anonymity and false pretense of having perceived freedom to say things to others you wouldn't say in person. No matter the time period, The Golden Rule still applies! 
Making progress is not without enough hardship already, let the difficulty be in overcoming the impossible, not each other.

replies.author: @jsoswell

Also, you have Never gone out of your way to insult people here,
In fact,, quite the opposite, you are always kind and understanding!!

Please keep on commenting and questioning, you are part of a Community  We are all Learning together!!
